title: Introduction to Climate Change & Sustainable Development

prerequisites.text: Acceptance for EGS Honours and by agreement of the course convenor.

outline: This course provides a broad, integrated knowledge on key issues in climate change and sustainable development, making students conversant across the spectrum of climate change issues and history. Topics include: sustainable development; the climate system, anthropogenic forcing and climate system response; African climate variability and change; international climate change legal frameworks, negotiations and politics; the economics of climate change and climate change financing; and the concept of climate compatible development. The course is lecture, seminar and group-work based, with each section involving basic framing lectures supported by essay or group work exercises and seminars.
